papers.title_en: Transformation Behavior of Images in Latent Space
papers.authors: Christian Zöllner, Mozzam Motiwala, Aysel Ahadova, Gerrit Anders, Robert Hüneburg, Jacob Nattermann, Matthias Kloor
papers.evidence: 研究病理组织学预训练编码器在图像变换下的潜在空间嵌入
papers.tldr: 组织病理图像分类依赖编码器提取特征，但不同编码器（通用预训练vs病理专用）在潜空间对数据增强中图像变换的鲁棒性缺乏比较。本文利用Lunit、Bioptimus的病理专用网络和Meta的通用网络，对结直肠和TCGA H&E切片施加标准变换，通过嵌入距离分析变换行为。结果发现，变换图像嵌入与原始图像嵌入的距离远小于随机嵌入，证明编码器对常见变换具有鲁棒性，但并非完全不变；同时，病理专用网络与通用网络在潜空间中保留的变换信息存在显著差异。这解释了为何数据增强仍能提升模型性能，并强调应根据下游任务选择或微调编码网络。
papers.abstract_en: Training of neural networks for histopathology classification tasks typically relies on data encoding into latent space, which reduces complexity and improves performance. There are several encoder networks available, either pretrained on general image datasets such as ImageNET, or specifically on histopathological images. Training of encoder networks should be adapted to downstream tasks, allowing encoding of biologic/diagnostic content while rendering networks invariant to label-irrelevant transformations.   This paper investigates the effect of classical image transformation on the latent space, using networks provided by Lunit Inc. and Bioptimus, both focusing on pathological images, and by Meta Research Team. We assess variance of embeddings resulting from standard data transformations by comparing original and transformed image embeddings and by contrasting them with random, unrelated embeddings, using image tiles from hematoxylin/eosin-stained sections available in a colorectal tissue dataset and the publicly accessible TCGA dataset.   Our findings show that embeddings of original and transformed images are closer to each other than to random embeddings, indicating robustness to transformations. However, they are not fully invariant, revealing that the encoder networks do not completely neutralize transformation effects in latent space, explaining why transformation-mediated augmentation of datasets can improve performance. Significant differences were observed between general and histopathology-specific encoder networks.
